questions.question: Define nested structure in C. How does it differ from a simple structure?
keyPoints: Nested structure is a structure containing another structure as its member
Enables modeling of hierarchical/has-a relationships
Simple structure contains only primitive data types or arrays as members
Nested allows grouping related composite data together

questions.question: Write the syntax to declare and initialize a nested structure for storing student information including name, roll number, and subject marks for 3 subjects.
keyPoints: Define inner structure for marks (3 subject integers)
Define outer structure for student (name, roll, marks structure)
Initialize using nested braces: {"Name", rollNo, {m1, m2, m3}}
Show example initialization with sample values

questions.question: Explain how to access members of a nested structure using both dot (.) and arrow (->) operators with an example.
keyPoints: Dot operator used when accessing through structure variable
Arrow operator used when accessing through pointer to structure
Show example: struct Emp e; e.address.city (dot)
For pointer: struct Emp *p=&e; p->address.city (arrow)

questions.question: What is the difference between nested structures and self-referential structures? Give suitable examples.
keyPoints: Nested: contains complete structure as member (composition/has-a)
Self-referential: contains pointer to same structure type (link relationship)
Example nested: struct Employee { struct Date dob; };
Example self-referential: struct Node { int data; struct Node *next; };

questions.question: How is memory allocated for nested structures? Explain with an example showing memory layout.
keyPoints: Nested structures allocate contiguous memory for all members
Outer structure's memory block includes inner structure's complete memory
Example: struct A { int x; } + struct B { struct A a; int y; }
Memory layout: x (4 bytes) followed by a.x (4 bytes) followed by y (4 bytes)

questions.question: Write a C program to declare a nested structure representing a book with title, author (first name, last name), and publication year. Initialize one variable and display all details.
keyPoints: Define struct Author with fname and lname
Define struct Book with title, year, and author structure
Initialize using nested braces
Use nested dot operator to display all details

questions.question: Explain the concept of anonymous nested structure in C with an example. What are its advantages?
keyPoints: Anonymous nested structure has no tag name, defined inside outer structure
Members accessed directly without intermediate name
Syntax: struct Outer { struct { int x; } ; } o; o.x directly
Advantage: reduces verbosity, simpler access to members

questions.question: Create a C program to manage employee records using nested structures. Each employee should have: personal details (name, age), department (dept name, dept code), and salary information (basic, hra, da). Write functions to: (i) Accept employee data (ii) Display employee details (iii) Calculate total salary.
keyPoints: Define nested structures for personal, department, salary
Main structure containing all nested structures
Function prototypes with appropriate parameter passing
Implementation showing nested member access and calculations

questions.question: Write a C program to store and display date of birth of 5 students using nested structures. The structure should include day, month, and year as separate members. Implement functions to find the oldest student and display all students born in a specific month.
keyPoints: Define Date structure
Define Student structure with nested Date for DOB and name
Array of 5 student structures
Functions for accepting, displaying, finding oldest, filtering by month
Demonstrate use of nested structure members in logic

questions.question: Explain passing nested structures to functions with examples. Write a C program demonstrating pass by value and pass by reference of nested structures.
keyPoints: Pass by value: entire structure copied to function
Pass by reference: address passed using & operator
Show prototype differences: void display(struct Date d) vs void display(struct Date *d)
Demonstrate arrow operator usage in pass by reference
Compare memory and performance implications

questions.question: Design a C program using nested structures to represent a complex number in rectangular form (real and imaginary parts). Implement functions to add, subtract, and multiply two complex numbers returning the result as a nested structure.
keyPoints: Define Complex structure with real and imaginary members
Function for addition: (a+bi) + (c+di) = (a+c) + (b+d)i
Function for subtraction: (a+bi) - (c+di) = (a-c) + (b-d)i
Function for multiplication: (a+bi)*(c+di) = (ac-bd) + (ad+bc)i
Return nested structure from functions and display results
